title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward has 12 stolen bases and 10 hits in 12 spring training games, impressing with base running skills.
Max Ferguson leads the Red Sox with 7 RBIs this spring and shows positional versatility for potential call-ups.
Tayron Guerrero has 6 strikeouts and allowed just one earned run in six innings, standing out in the bullpen.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson ranks second in 5-on-5 goals for the Bruins this season with 13 goals.
Boston Bruins retained Arvidsson despite trade offers, valuing his experience and scoring ability.
Arvidsson's potential contract extension could bring stability to Bruins' middle-six forwards beyond 2026.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears, potentially moving Jared Wilson to center from left guard.
Isaac Seumalo is a top free-agent option for Patriots, with projected one-year, $8 million deal.
Elgton Jenkins, a potential Packers cut, could provide flexibility at left guard or center for Patriots.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, 48, from Stockton, California, is participating in the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Perry runs to support Make-A-Wish children in the Greater Massachusetts-Rhode Island region.
He has been involved with Make-A-Wish for 15 years, inspired by the children's resilience and hope.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots expected to pursue edge rusher Boye Mafe in free agency, with a projected contract of three years, $48 million.
Mafe, 27, logged 44 pressures and a 17.4% pass rush win rate with the Seahawks last season.
Patriots unlikely to sign Colts wide receiver Alec Pierce, who is projected to join Chargers for $80 million.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at Boston's House of Blues on March 6, 2026, with sharp political commentary and covers.
Welles, nominated for four Grammy awards in 2026, sang about recent events using satirical lyrics and a southern twang.
The setlist included originals like 'Sometimes You Bomb Iran' and covers like 'Take Me Home, Country Roads' by John Denver.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and had three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea in the World Baseball Classic.
Yoshida's performance included a 387-foot homer in the third inning and a two-run single in the seventh inning.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game in Tokyo while wearing a Dodgers uniform.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, a 25-year-old from Roslindale, is running her first Boston Marathon in 2026.
She is supporting Mass. Eye and Ear, where she was treated for eye conditions over the last decade.
Mass. Eye and Ear specializes in eye, ear, nose, and throat conditions and offers a 24-hour emergency department.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler joined ESPN in 2000 and is now a color analyst on its top NBA team for the 2025-26 season.
Legler, Mike Breen, and Richard Jefferson will call the NBA Finals, with sideline reporter Lisa Salters on ABC.
ESPN promoted Legler after Doc Rivers and JJ Redick left for coaching roles, and Doris Burke moved to the No. 2 team.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Jayson Tatum scored 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists in his return from an Achilles injury after 298 days.
Cooper Flagg contributed 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists for the Mavericks in his TD Garden debut.
Flagg, a Maine native and No. 1 draft pick, received strong support from fans, despite playing against the Celtics.
